data.title: XRP aims to break $1.73 resistance for February rally; trading at $1.60. Potential breakout to $2.60-$4.00 range.
data.description: The current trading price of XRP stands at $1.60 with a neutral Relative Strength Index (RSI) and a significant resistance level at $1.73. Technical analysis indicates a potential breakout towards the range of $2.60 to $4.00 if bulls manage to regain momentum.

data.title: Record number of 476,000 Americans now have two full-time jobs, marking significant labor market shift.
data.description: The latest data from The Kobeissi Letter reveals that the number of Americans juggling two full-time jobs has surged to 476,000. This figure is the second-highest level ever recorded, just shy of the all-time peak of 488,000 seen in December 2025. This trend marks a significant shift in the U.S. labor market, with dual full-time employment having doubled since 2020 and surpassing the previous peak of 416,000 during the dot-com bubble in July 2000. This unprecedented rise in Americans holding two primary full-time positions illustrates the changing dynamics of the workforce. For more details, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Senators request Bessent to investigate $500M UAE investment in Trump-linked WLFI, citing national security fears.
data.description: Elizabeth Warren and Andy Kim are urging Treasury’s Scott Bessent to investigate a UAE-funded investment in a crypto company linked to Trump due to national security worries.

data.title: BNB price may reach $680-$720 by March 2026, showing oversold conditions at $638 with RSI at 31.52. Technical analysis indicates a potential recovery.
data.description: The current price of Binance Coin (BNB) at $638 indicates oversold conditions, as reflected by a Relative Strength Index (RSI) of 31.52. Based on technical analysis, it is predicted that BNB may aim for a range between $680 and $720 if it surpasses key resistance levels.

data.title: Ethereum targets $2,400 recovery in short-term; forecast $2,000-$2,800 range in 1 month with $2,151 as strong resistance.
data.description: Summary of Ethereum Price Projections: In the short term (1 week), the price target is between $2,400 and $2,500. Looking at the medium term (1 month), the forecast falls within the range of $2,000 to $2,800. A key level to watch for a bullish breakout is $2,151, which acts as a strong resistance point.

data.title: Bitcoin price aims for $75,000 in March 2026, currently trading near $70,424 with bullish indicators.
data.description: Bitcoin is currently trading close to $70,424, displaying mixed signals. Technical indicators hint at a possible surge towards the $75,000 resistance level in the near future. The BTC price forecast indicates a bullish trend with a potential short-term increase.

data.title: Roundhill’s election forecast ETFs are 'innovative': Analyst warns choosing wrong fund connected to US presidential race could lead to capital loss.
data.description: Investors are being cautioned by the ETF issuer that choosing the fund linked to the losing US presidential outcome may result in a loss of almost all of their invested capital.

data.title: Institutions could fire Bitcoin devs due to quantum risks: VC warns of corporate takeover if not addressed quickly.
data.description: Bitcoin developers need to swiftly address the quantum threats facing Bitcoin in order to prevent a potential takeover by corporations, warned venture capitalist Nic Carter.

data.title: Dow Jones index drops in market sell-off. S&P 500 turns negative for 2026. Cisco and AppLovin stocks plummet.
data.description: Today's stock market experienced a significant decline as the Dow Jones index plummeted during a market sell-off on Thursday. Tech companies AppLovin and Cisco saw a sharp decline in their stock prices following their earnings reports. The S&P 500 also turned negative for the year 2026. The market showed Live Coverage of the situation as the Dow Jones dived and both Cisco and AppLovin cratered in terms of their financial standing.

data.title: Americans split on 2026 economy: 50% anticipate stock market gains, 49% predict economic growth, 62% foresee inflation climb, 50% higher joblessness. Sentiment recovering from tariff-driven pessimism in 2025.
data.description: In January 2026, Americans began the year with cautious optimism about the economy and stock market. According to Gallup's economic sentiment poll conducted from Jan. 2-17, 50% of U.S. adults anticipate stock market gains and 49% forecast economic growth. However, concerns remain with 62% expecting inflation to rise and half anticipating higher unemployment rates. This shows a divided sentiment among the population. These results indicate a rebound from April 2025 when optimism plummeted due to tariffs imposed by President Donald Trump. During that time, only 29% expected stock market gains, which has since risen by 21 percentage points. Similarly, the belief in economic growth has increased from 38% to 49%. Now, only 25% predict a decline in equities in the next six months, and 36% expect a shrinking economy. This change reflects a more positive outlook as the markets have stabilized. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Stock market sees mixed close with Dow hitting new record high, Nasdaq streak broken.
data.description: On Tuesday, the stock market experienced mixed results with the major indexes closing after a volatile day. The Dow Jones Industrial Average achieved a new record high, while the Nasdaq ended its winning streak. The fluctuating market trend was observed throughout the day, leading to a diverse performance in the indexes. The Dow closed at a record number, showcasing the strength of certain stocks, while the Nasdaq faced a minor setback. This ongoing turbulence in the market highlights the current unpredictability and challenges being faced by investors.

data.title: US prosecutors caution about romance scams tied to cryptocurrencies before Valentine’s Day, often involving "pig-butchering" schemes.
data.description: Numerous cases are now linked to schemes involving "pig-butchering", which are elaborate scams combining emotional manipulation with fraudulent cryptocurrency platforms.

data.title: U.S. faces pivotal January jobs report Wednesday, including revisions with high stakes; forecasts suggest 45,000 increase in nonfarm payrolls and 4.4% unemployment rate.
data.description: The U.S. labor market awaits the January jobs report, to be released at 8:30 a.m. ET, which includes crucial benchmarks and revisions. The report is significant due to ongoing debates about the market's momentum. Bank of America economist Shruti Mishra dubbed it as the "Super Bowl of jobs reports" due to its importance. Along with January payrolls and unemployment rates, traders will analyze revisions to previous data. Bank of America predicts a 45,000 rise in nonfarm payrolls, lower than consensus, with an expected unemployment rate of 4.4%. This projection accounts for potential adjustments to the birth-death model. The report holds added weight due to its potential impact on market narratives.

data.title: Inspira Technologies receives Nasdaq notification for not meeting minimum bid price, shares below $1.00 for 30 days.
data.description: Inspira™ Technologies OXY B.H.N. Ltd., based in Ra'anana, Israel, revealed that they have received a Notification Letter from Nasdaq on February 9, 2026. The letter stated that the company, listed under NASDAQ as IINN and IINNW, is not meeting the minimum bid price requirement outlined in Nasdaq Listing Rule 5550(a)(2). This is due to the closing bid price of the company's Ordinary Shares falling below $1.00 per share for the past 30 consecutive business days.

data.title: Experts predict Bitcoin nearing turning point, but no clear bottom yet. Valuation metrics still above past cycle lows.
data.description: Important valuation indicators are currently not as low as they have been during previous cycles, with inflation risks continuing to impact digital assets.

data.title: Stingray to have single ticker for TSX trading starting Feb. 13, 2026. World's top streaming media company.
data.description: Montreal-based Stingray Group Inc. declared today that its subordinate voting shares and variable subordinate voting shares will consolidate under one ticker on the Toronto Stock Exchange starting February 13, 2026. As the global leader in connected streaming media, the company's move aims to streamline trading of its shares on the TSX.

data.title: CFTC adds top crypto execs to advisory panel during market structure debate on new rules for digital-asset markets.
data.description: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) has appointed top cryptocurrency executives to its advisory panel amidst discussions on implementing new regulations for digital asset markets.

data.title: Alaska car wash offers breakup letters as part of Valentine's Day campaign, customers embrace the unique idea.
data.description: In Anchorage, Alaska on February 14, 2026, CYBER Express Wash took a unique approach to Valentine's Day by offering breakup letters instead of traditional romantic gestures. The car wash company initiated a campaign called "Breakups Are Hard. We'll Handle the Paperwork." which involves sending real USPS certified breakup letters to recipients.

data.title: Roundhill's ETFs for US election are groundbreaking, but risky. Picking losing candidate could mean losing most of investment.
data.description: Investors have been cautioned by the ETF issuer that choosing the fund associated with the unsuccessful US presidential outcome could lead to the potential loss of almost all invested capital.

data.title: Dow Jones Futures pose danger; strategies to mitigate risks discussed with focus on Apple, Google, Amazon, Meta, and Nvidia.
data.description: Methods to mitigate the dangers of a volatile market are being discussed amidst the current market conditions. Companies such as Apple, Google, Amazon, Meta, and Nvidia are displaying resilience despite challenges. Investors are advised to consider the situation with Dow Jones Futures. This information has been shared in a recent report by Investor's Business Daily.
